famous quote:
If you want to be an anomoly, you gotta act like one.
-Gary Vaynerchuk
Interview Text:
Dave and Emily:

Dave and Emily are friends from college. They got married and had a daughter they named Hazel. When she was only two years old she was diagnosed with cancer. After a long tortuous battle with it Hazel passed away. 

In the first release of the Ethiopia Book I asked people if they would give me corrections. Emily read and gave me some great corrections but she also wrote this story on an open page, after the story of a man who God had given 15 extra years of life.

"We prayed so much for Hazel's healing. We had meetings with church elders in which she was prayed over. I even tried commanding the cancer (demon?) out of Hazel in the name of Jesus. 

Althought Hazel's cancer was not ever removed from her body once it returned, we did once see something that cannot be scientifically explained. 

After her first MRI, her oncologist told us that it appeared that Hazel's cancer had metastasized around her pituitary gland. This would be very bad news. She had another MRI 3 days later after her surgery to remove the bulk tumor (not around her pituitary gland). The apparent metastasis was gone. We were overjoyed and called it a miracle. I thought maybe it was a sign that God was on Hazel's side. 

Of course, her metastasis returned in her spine 14 months later. But I still wonder about our "miracle." A metastasis around the pituitary would mean just weeks left. Hazel had two years and nine months after her diagnosis. I think about the story of the man who was granted "15 more years," and our hospital chaplin's advice that "everyone who has had a miraculous healing dies eventually." Between modern medicine and perhaps an outright miracle, Hazel had years that she otherwise would not have had.
Music Video Instructions: Imagine this as God calling out to you, missing you, wanting you back.
